name: Perspective API (Google Jigsaw)
pricing_notes: Free for non-commercial / research; commercial use requires approval and may incur costs

auth.notes: Google Cloud API key required; quota by request

performance.notes: REST API; typically 150-200ms for text analysis

agent_readiness.notes: Toxicity scoring for text. Attributes: TOXICITY, SEVERE_TOXICITY, IDENTITY_ATTACK, INSULT, PROFANITY, THREAT. Returns probability scores 0-1 per attribute. Used by Wikipedia, NYT comments. Limited to English primarily.

gotchas: Heavily English-biased; other languages less reliable
No prompt injection or jailbreak detection
Doesn't cover categories like misinformation or spam
Rate limits: 1 QPS default (can request increase)
best_when: User-generated content moderation at community scale (comments, forums)
Research on online toxicity and hate speech detection
Augmenting agent outputs with toxicity scoring before delivery
summary: Perspective API (Google Jigsaw) detects toxic content with probability scores across toxicity, identity attacks, insults, threats, and profanity. Used by Wikipedia and NYT for comment moderation. Free but commercial use requires approval. English-centric. Not designed for LLM-specific threats (prompt injection, jailbreaks). Best for community/UGC moderation use cases.
